user_gap: KDT OS detected API / Routes in this uploaded project and created a ready practice quest.
title: API Weather Lookup App
goal: Practice API requests, JSON parsing, button events, loading state, and error handling.
what_to_build: A browser app with index.html, style.css, and script.js where a button fetches sample weather JSON and displays city, temperature, and condition.
steps: In index.html add an input with id cityInput
Add a button with id searchBtn
Add a div with id result
Link script.js at the bottom of the HTML body
In script.js select cityInput, searchBtn, and result
Add a click event listener to searchBtn
Inside the click handler set result.textContent to Loading...
Use fetch() to request sample JSON or a real weather endpoint
Use response.json() to parse the data
Display city, temperature, and condition inside result
Add catch() or try/catch to show an error message
Open index.html and test the button
Create README.md explaining which API or sample JSON you used
success_criteria: result area changes after the request
Error text appears if request fails
proof_required: Include screenshot of result area after clicking

teach_me_breakdown.label: What is an API?
teach_me_breakdown.answer: An API is a way for one app to ask another app or service for data.

teach_me_breakdown.label: What is fetch()?
teach_me_breakdown.answer: fetch() is JavaScript's way to make a web request from the browser.

teach_me_breakdown.label: What is JSON?
teach_me_breakdown.answer: JSON is a data format that looks like JavaScript objects and is commonly returned by APIs.

teach_me_breakdown.label: What is an endpoint?
teach_me_breakdown.answer: An endpoint is the URL you request, like /weather or /api/tasks.

resume_rule: After this quest is complete, submit proof, update skill evidence, then return to the project or goal that caused this quest.
